content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the milestone lifecycle/journal changes in `src/contexts/milestone_record/{model,service}.rs`, `src/adapters/fs.rs`, and the run-sync integration in `src/cli/run.rs`. Ran targeted tests (`cargo test update_status_`, `cargo test resume_transition_`, `cargo test completion_after_missed_start_reconstructs_running_bridge`); they pass, but I found two correctness gaps around failed-state reachability and repair transitions.

## Amendments

1. Handle unrecoverable run failures as a real `running -> failed` milestone transition on the normal sync path. `src/cli/run.rs:180-188` maps a failed project run to `TaskRunOutcome::Failed`, and `src/cli/run.rs:346-360` always sends that through `record_bead_completion`. But `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:916-923` reconciles any running milestone with no active bead into `Paused`, so the failed run path never leaves the milestone in `Failed` and never emits the required `running -> failed` lifecycle event. Add a way for the completion/sync flow to distinguish retryable bead failure from unrecoverable milestone failure and commit the `Failed` transition atomically with the journal update.
   Rationale: The new `failed` lifecycle is effectively unreachable from actual run failures, so the system cannot track milestone failure the way the bead requires.
2. Fix `repair_task_run` so it can heal a paused milestone into `Completed` after correcting a stale failed attempt. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2046-2083`, repairing the lineage can legitimately recompute the snapshot as completed via `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:909-915`, but `build_reconciled_transition_events` only synthesizes bridge transitions from `Planning` or `Ready` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:708-734`). A `Paused -> Completed` repair therefore fails with an invalid-transition error instead of repairing the milestone. The repair path needs to synthesize the missing running bridge (or otherwise replace the stale paused state) so stale terminal rows can be corrected end to end.
   Rationale: This breaks the intended self-healing flow: once a bad failed completion leaves the milestone paused, repairing that attempt to succeeded/skipped cannot bring the milestone to the correct terminal state.
